Details

What are dbt meetups? dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/ and make sure to add the #local-berlin channel in dbt Slack to be part of the conversation.
🤝 Organizers: Torsten Glunde (DDVUG), Stephan Durry (dbt Labs), Lucas Silbernagel (dbt Labs)
🏢 Venue: Jugendherberge Berlin Ostkreuz - Marktstraße 9-12, 10317 Berlin

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

📝Agenda

6:00 PM: Check in/Registration
6:30 PM: Welcome Remarks 🎤 (Torsten Glunde, DDVUG)
6:40 PM: What’s new with dbt? 🎤 (Stephan Durry + Lucas Silbernagel, dbt Labs)
6:55 PM: 1. Talk: Model-driven Platform automation with dbt 🎤 (Torsten Glunde, DDVUG)
7:15 PM: Break
7:30 PM: 2. Talk: From Events to Decisions - Data Engineering in a Blue-Collar Hiring Marketplace 🎤 (Shpat Cheliku + Jerry Nwabuilo, PowerUs)
7:50 PM: Networking & Reception with Food and Drinks

Closing doors at 6:40

The Berlin dbt Meetup is always booked out within a few hours - it's amazing to see how many people from the dbt community want to attend. To give those that are on the waitlist a chance to attend, please update your RSVP if your plans change.
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Speaker Lineup

1. Marielle Dado + Eva Schreyer with

How to Win Friends and Influence People When Migrating to dbt
Introducing new tools into your company's tech stack can be a difficult and sometimes frustrating process. Based on our experiences, we've noticed that analysts and analytics engineers often have different ideas about what constitutes "better", "faster", and "quality" when it comes to data. In this talk, we discuss the do's and don'ts of dbt migration from two different perspectives: that of Marielle, an analytics engineer who introduced dbt and cloud data warehousing to the tech stack of two companies and always believed in their value to data teams, and Eva, a data analyst who initially disliked working with dbt but grew to love it over time. Ultimately, our goal is to help you win friends and influence people with a frictionless migration to dbt or other new tools in your tech stack.

2. Mikkel Dengsøe with

Improving your Data Alerting Workflow
Managing the alerting workflow is no easy task as you scale. On the one hand you want to know about issues as they happen but at the same time you don’t want to end up flooded with data alerts that don’t get actioned. In this talk I'll dive into different type of dbt metadata you can surface in alerts such as ownership, importance and root cause analysis that can help you prioritise, resolve and debug issues faster.

✅ Agenda

6:30 pm - 6:35 pm: Welcome Remarks by Bijan&Ben
6:35 pm - 6:50 pm: Data Exploration and Visualization in the Modern Data Stack, Andras Zimmer
6:50 pm - 7:00 pm: Audience Q&A with Andras
7:00 pm - 7:15 pm: The Role of Computational Notebooks in the Modern Data Stack, Jakub Zitny
7:15 pm - 7:25 pm: Audience Q&A with Jakub
7:25 pm - 7:30 pm: Closing Remarks by Bijan&Ben

-----------------------------------
✨ Speaker Lineup ✨

🎤 Data Exploration and Visualization in the Modern Data Stack
🤝Andras Zimmer, CTO, Hiflylabs

Andras, as the CTO of a data-centric consulting firm, is a passionate advocate of the Modern Data Stack. In this talk, he will concentrate on the “upper side” of the stack, explaining when Superset can be an ideal open-source dashboarding companion to dbt, but also mentioning its downsides/limitations and some dbt best practices. The demo he brings will analyze the current dbt and data engineering related job offerings, so you can expect some special insights, like a comparison of Germany/Europe vs USA.

🎤 The Role of Computational Notebooks in the Modern Data Stack
👨‍💻 Jakub Zitny, Product Engineer, Deepnote

Jakub and the Deepnote team are working on the next generation of collaborative notebooks in the cloud that play well with the Modern Data Stack. They have been asking the question: How do notebooks fit into different data workflows? Data science notebooks are usually suited for tasks that require rapid iteration, the flexibility of scripting languages, and in-place visualizations, but can we do more? How might notebooks empower data engineers in middle-sized and large companies? What does the ETL/ELT processes look like in 2021 and beyond, and how can we simplify the entry barrier for a wider group of employees in and outside of the data teams?

🎤Host: Bijan&Ben, Co-founder and data enthusiast, Gemma Analytics

-----------------------------------
